012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,

015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.rest.client;
018
019import java.io.*;
020import java.nio.charset.*;
021import java.util.*;
022
023import org.apache.juneau.rest.client.assertion.*;
024
025/**
026 * An HTTP response returned by {@link RestRequest#run()}.
027 *
028 * <p>
029 * Wraps the transport-layer {@link TransportResponse} and provides higher-level accessors.
030 * Callers <b>must</b> close this response (it implements {@link Closeable}) to release transport resources.
031 *
032 * <p>
033 * <b>Beta — API subject to change:</b> This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack
034 * ({@code org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*}).
035 * It is not API-frozen: binary- and source-incompatible changes may appear in the <b>next major</b> Juneau release
036 * (and possibly earlier).
037 *
038 * @since 9.2.1
039 */
040@SuppressWarnings({
041   "resource" // response is owned and closed via RestResponse.close(); getBodyStream() transfers ownership to caller
042})
043public final class RestResponse implements Closeable {
044
045   private final TransportResponse response;
046   private final RestClient client;
047
048   RestResponse(TransportResponse response, RestClient client) {
049      this.response = response;
050      this.client = client;
051   }
052
053   /**
054    * Returns the client that produced this response.
055    *
056    * <p>
057    * <b>Beta — API subject to change:</b> This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack
058    * ({@code org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*}).
059    * It is not API-frozen: binary- and source-incompatible changes may appear in the <b>next major</b> Juneau release
060    * (and possibly earlier).
061    *
062    * @return The client that produced this response. Never <jk>null</jk>.
063    */
064   public RestClient getClient() {
065      return client;
066   }
067
068   /**
069    * Returns the HTTP status code.
070    *
071    * @return The status code (e.g. {@code 200}).
072    */
073   public int getStatusCode() {
074      return response.getStatusCode();
075   }
076
077   /**
078    * Returns the reason phrase (e.g. {@code "OK"}), or {@code null} if absent.
079    *
080    * @return The reason phrase, possibly <jk>null</jk>.
081    */
082   public String getReasonPhrase() {
083      return response.getReasonPhrase();
084   }
085
086   /**
087    * Returns all response headers.
088    *
089    * @return An unmodifiable list. Never <jk>null</jk>.
090    */
091   public List<TransportHeader> getHeaders() {
092      return response.getHeaders();
093   }
094
095   /**
096    * Returns the first response header with the given name (case-insensitive), or {@code null} if absent.
097    *
098    * @param name The header name. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
099    * @return The first matching header, or <jk>null</jk>.
100    */
101   public TransportHeader getFirstHeader(String name) {
102      return response.getFirstHeader(name);
103   }
104
105   /**
106    * Returns the response body as a UTF-8 string, or {@code null} if there is no body.
107    *
108    * @return The body as a string, possibly <jk>null</jk>.
109    * @throws IOException If an I/O error occurs reading the body.
110    */
111   public String getBodyAsString() throws IOException {
112      var body = response.getBody();
113      if (body == null)
114         return null;
115      return new String(body.readAllBytes(), StandardCharsets.UTF_8);
116   }
117
118   /**
119    * Returns the raw response body stream, or {@code null} if there is no body.
120    *
121    * <p>
122    * Callers must not close this stream directly — close the {@link RestResponse} instead.
123    *
124    * @return The body stream, possibly <jk>null</jk>.
125    */
126   public InputStream getBodyStream() {
127      return response.getBody();
128   }
129
130   /**
131    * Asserts that the status code is in the 2xx range.
132    *
133    * @return This object (for chaining).
134    * @throws RestCallException If the status code is not 2xx.
135    */
136   public RestResponse assertOk() throws RestCallException {
137      var sc = response.getStatusCode();
138      if (sc < 200 || sc > 299)
139         throw new RestCallException(sc, "Expected 2xx status but got " + sc + " " + response.getReasonPhrase());
140      return this;
141   }
142
143   /**
144    * Asserts that the status code equals the expected value.
145    *
146    * @param expected The expected status code.
147    * @return This object (for chaining).
148    * @throws RestCallException If the status code does not match.
149    */
150   public RestResponse assertStatus(int expected) throws RestCallException {
151      var actual = response.getStatusCode();
152      if (actual != expected)
153         throw new RestCallException(actual, "Expected status " + expected + " but got " + actual);
154      return this;
155   }
156
157   /**
158    * Returns a fluent body accessor for this response.
159    *
160    * @return A new body accessor. Never <jk>null</jk>.
161    */
162   public ResponseBody body() {
163      return new ResponseBody(this);
164   }
165
166   /**
167    * Returns a fluent assertion object for this response.
168    *
169    * <p>
170    * Use this for test-style validation of the response in production or test code:
171    * <p class='bjava'>
172    *    <jv>resp</jv>.assertThat()
173    *       .statusCode(200)
174    *       .body().contains(<js>"alice"</js>);
175    * </p>
176    *
177    * @return A new assertion object. Never <jk>null</jk>.
178    */
179   public ResponseAssertion assertThat() {
180      return new ResponseAssertion(this);
181   }
182
183   /**
184    * Returns a fluent header accessor for the named response header.
185    *
186    * @param name The header name (case-insensitive). Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
187    * @return A new header accessor. Never <jk>null</jk>.
188    */
189   public ResponseHeader header(String name) {
190      return new ResponseHeader(name, this);
191   }
192
193   @Override /* Closeable */
194   public void close() throws IOException {
195      response.close();
196   }
197}
